1.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
1.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

7.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

8.gl: a good message, i.e. the gospel

9.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
9.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

9.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
9.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

10.gl: glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

10.dv: feminine of a derivative of G4990 (σωτήρ) as (properly, abstract) noun;

10.gl: perpetual (also used of past time, or past and future as well)

14.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

15.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
15.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

15.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
15.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

17.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
17.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

18.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)
18.kj: raised to life again, resurrection, rise from the dead, that should rise, rising again

19.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
19.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

22.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
22.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
22.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);

22.gl: the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle

24.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

25.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
25.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
